D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Red and Arousal — Sympathetic Activation

Saturated red elevates heart rate and salivary cortisol vs neutral grey in some studies (Elliot 2010); sports-uniform meta-analysis shows ~3% win advantage for red teams

Hill & Barton (2005) reanalysed Olympic combat sports: red-uniform athletes won 55-60% of matched bouts when contestants were closely matched. Mechanism debated — testosterone signalling, opponent intimidation, or referee bias. Effect is small and culturally moderated.

Slide stimulus saturation to see modelled arousal response.
